description: Analyze a decade of US Census American Community Survey data for Austin, Texas from ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/austin_census_tracts.csv to calculate growth metrics, create a composite gentrification index, filter out statistical noise, and extract the most rapidly changing neighborhoods into a summary report. Add four calculated columns to the dataset: Income_Growth ((Med_Inc_2022 - Med_Inc_2012) / Med_Inc_2012), Rent_Growth ((Med_Rent_2022 - Med_Rent_2012) / Med_Rent_2012), Edu_Growth ((Edu_2022 - Edu_2012) / Edu_2012), and Gentrification_Index (the sum of the three growth metrics). Filter the dataset to exclude census tracts where Total_Pop_2022 is less than 500 and sort the remaining tracts by Gentrification_Index in descending order. Create a new sheet named 'Top_10_Gentrified' containing the header and the top 10 rows from the sorted, filtered dataset. On the original data sheet, include a final row calculating the average for Med_Inc_2012, Med_Inc_2022, Med_Rent_2012, and Med_Rent_2022 across all tracts. Save the completed workbook to ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/austin_gentrification_analysis.xlsx.
